preamble: The replacement of the vowel [ʌ] by a glottal stop [ʔ] is attested in extremely reduced or clipped speech. More commonly, [ʌ] undergoes glottal reinforcement — a glottal stop is prepended (hard attack) rather than substituting the vowel. Full replacement (ʌ→ʔ) occurs in fast speech elision or certain Cockney-type coda glottalization patterns.

examples.note: Glottal onset (hard attack): a glottal stop precedes the vowel; the vowel is reinforced, not replaced.

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Johnson, K. (2015). A Course in Phonetics (7th ed.). Cengage Learning.
